title: Bulga laxmannii
intro: Bulga laxmannii, also known as Laxmann's bulga, is a type of plant in the Bulga genus that is commonly used in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Bulga laxmannii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Bulga laxmannii is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, coughs, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Bulga laxmannii is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Australia. The classification of Bulga laxmannii is within the genus Bulga.
